title: Careers
text:
We are seeking a R&D S&E, Electrical Engineering
What Your Job Will Be Like
The Weapon Interface & Controllers Department is seeking an enthusiastic, self-motivated Electrical Engineer to join our dynamic, high-impact team. In this role, you will play a critical part in the design, troubleshooting, testing, qualification, and evaluation of weapon controller based electronic systems, which are the brains of a nuclear weapon, as well as supporting existing production systems. This position offers a unique opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge nuclear deterrence programs that demand exceptional performance, reliability, and rigor. The role emphasizes hands-on implementation, system-level integration, and continuous improvement of design and qualification.
As a successful candidate, you will collaborate closely across multidisciplinary teams to deliver robust electrical design and qualification solutions. You will actively engage in the design and trade space assessment of weapon interface and controller type of products. You will collaborate closely with the Kansas City National Security Campus (KCNSC) Production Agency (PA), Sandia Design Agency (DA) PRT, and Subject Matter Experts (SME) to understand and influence functional and interface requirements, design elements, qualification testing, analysis, and production capabilities. In addition, you will have the opportunity to influence and shape product realization strategy. This position is ideal for someone who values ownership, technical leadership, and continuous learning in a challenging and meaningful environment where no two days are the same.
Why Join Us
Our team thrives in a positive, inclusive environment where mentors and learners work side by side. We believe the best ideas emerge through collaboration, shared knowledge, and mutual respect, and we actively invest in each others technical growth and career development.
By joining Department 5357, you'll be part of a high-performing, mission-driven organization solving complex, meaningful engineering challenges that directly support national security. Your work will matter, your expertise will be valued, and your contributions will have real impact.
If you're seeking a role where your electrical engineering skills enable cutting-edge systems, where your voice and leadership are encouraged, and where you can grow alongside talented peers, we invite you to apply and build your future with us.
Due to the nature of the work, the selected applicant must be able to work onsite.
Sandia is required by DOE to conduct a pre-employment drug test and background review that includes checks of personal references, credit, law enforcement records, and employment/education verifications. Applicants for employment need to be able to obtain and maintain a DOE Q-level security clearance, which requires U.S. citizenship. If you hold more than one citizenship (i.e., of the U.S. and another country), your ability to obtain a security clearance may be impacted.
Applicants offered employment with Sandia are subject to a federal background investigation to meet the requirements for access to classified information or matter if the duties of the position require a DOE security clearance. Substance abuse or illegal drug use, falsification of information, criminal activity, serious misconduct or other indicators of untrustworthiness can cause a clearance to be denied or terminated by DOE, resulting in the inability to perform the duties assigned and subsequent termination of employment.
